key_files.purpose: Deployment and main operator runbook for Vercel, domains, and env configuration.
Next.js catch-all page that mounts the React SPA and injects SSR SEO metadata.
Client app shell, React Router route map, providers, auth context, and shared config loading.
Catch-all Next.js API entrypoint.
Route dispatcher that maps API paths and methods to handlers.
Main backend business logic for auth, PDFs, links, billing, domains, team workspaces, and admin features.
Supabase Postgres-backed document store abstraction built on app_documents/app_files.
Schema and indexes for app_documents/app_files plus JSONB collection indexes.
Runtime language resolution, translation overrides, and localization bootstrapping.
Shared authenticated layout, sidebar navigation, workspace switching, and account footer.
Secure PDF viewing surface with anti-copy deterrence, watermarking, NDA, focus lock, and timers.
Primary workspace for PDFs, folders, links, bulk actions, and secure-link editing.
Admin reporting dashboard including revenue, subscription, refund, and usage drilldowns.
Platform control surface wrapping grouped settings tabs.
Extracted admin settings tab UI bodies.
User account settings wrapper including billing, security, link defaults, domains, and team access.
Extracted end-user settings tab UI bodies.
Applies Supabase/Postgres schema migrations.
Seeds admin/sample users and starter platform data.
Vercel production build wrapper that can run DB migrations before next build.

architecture.next_config_summary: React SPA is mounted through pages/[[...slug]].jsx with same-origin API rewrites for /direct/:token.
architecture.prd_note: memory/PRD.md is historically useful but partially outdated. It still references the earlier FastAPI/Mongo architecture and should be treated as product history, not the active runtime design.
readme_summary: This repo is now configured so frontend and backend can run under one Vercel project:
- Next.js frontend at repo root (`pages/` + `frontend/src/` UI code)
1. Import this repository as a Vercel project.
- `NEXT_PUBLIC_BACKEND_URL` (leave empty to use same-origin `/api` on Vercel).
- Registration email verification and password reset emails are sent by Supabase Auth.
- In Supabase Dashboard, configure `Auth -> URL Configuration` with your app URL and redirect URLs (`/verify-email`, `/reset-password`).
- Legacy Resend fallback is still supported only if Supabase auth env vars are missing.
Use your primary platform domain (example: `securepdf.vikasbendha.com`) as the app origin.

items: Email/password auth
Google login and signup through Supabase OAuth
Email verification and password reset
Verified email change flow
Admin and super admin two-factor authentication (QR plus TOTP)
Role model: user, admin, super_admin
capabilities.name: Authentication and Identity

items: PDF upload and storage
Secure link generation with countdown, fixed date, and manual revoke modes
Direct access tokens for non-secure PDF viewing when enabled
Focus lock, idle timeout, fullscreen requirement, NDA gate, single-viewer session lock
Watermark modes: basic details, custom text, uploaded logo
IP allowlist, first-IP lock, country allow/block restrictions
Expired page customization and manual link revocation
capabilities.name: Secure PDF and Link Controls

items: Supabase Postgres-backed app_files storage provider
Wasabi S3-compatible storage provider
Admin storage settings with migration jobs and operations health
Same-origin /api deployment model on Vercel
capabilities.name: Storage and Delivery

items: Stripe subscriptions and checkout
Admin-managed plans with public visibility toggles and direct plan links
Billing portal support
Admin refund workflow with full and partial refund support
Admin revenue, subscription, and refund reporting with date-range filters
Invoice template customization and PDF invoice generation
capabilities.name: Billing, Plans, and Revenue

items: Supported languages: English, Spanish, French, German, Italian, Hindi, Slovenian
Platform default language plus per-user override
Admin manual translation manager with string overrides and advanced JSON editing
Editable branding, SEO, public-site, and auth email content from admin settings
capabilities.name: Localization and Content

items: User custom domains for secure and direct PDF links
DNS verification with TXT, CNAME, and apex A-record support
Optional Vercel API automation for domain attach and verification
SSL state tracking and enforcement before domain use
capabilities.name: Custom Domains and White Label

items: Owner/admin/member workspace roles for customer accounts
Team invitations with accept and decline flows
Workspace switching in the authenticated UI
Audit events for platform settings, PDFs, links, folders, billing, and team actions
Settings permissions, settings history, and background jobs for admin operations
capabilities.name: Team Workspaces and Auditing

npm_scripts.db:setup: npm run db:migrate && npm run db:seed

comments: Build-time DB migration behavior
On Vercel production deploys, migration runs by default.
Override with RUN_DB_MIGRATIONS_ON_BUILD=true/false.
Emergency flag to skip migration even when enabled
App security
Email delivery
Choose one:
- `supabase`: Supabase Auth sends reset/verification emails (configure custom SMTP in Supabase for production)
- `gmail`: Google OAuth mailer configured from the admin panel
- `mailgun`: Mailgun API mailer configured from the admin panel
- `outlook`: Microsoft 365 / Outlook OAuth mailer configured from the admin panel
- `smtp`: Other SMTP mailer configured below or overridden from the admin panel
- `resend`: this app sends emails using Resend env vars
Super admin can override SMTP settings from the admin panel at runtime.
Storage provider
Optional Stripe settings
Comma-separated frontend origins
Custom domain verification + DNS targets
Optional extra accepted CNAME targets, comma-separated
Optional A targets for apex domains, comma-separated

environment.note: This export includes placeholders and examples only. Real secrets must stay in local env files or Vercel/Supabase settings.

known_constraints: The active runtime is the root Next.js app. The historical backend/ directory may still exist in the repo, but the production deployment path is pages/ plus frontend/src/.
The app is a React SPA mounted inside a Next.js catch-all page, not a pure App Router Next.js app.
Screenshot prevention is deterrence-only. Browser code can increase friction, but cannot reliably block OS-level screenshots.
Countdown links are per public IP. Browsers or devices sharing the same public IP share the same countdown state for that link.
Billing and subscriptions are owner-account based. Team members act inside the owner's workspace but do not have separate subscription billing.
The document store uses JSONB collections inside public.app_documents, so schema evolution is mostly application-level rather than table-per-entity.
NEXT_PUBLIC_BACKEND_URL is usually left empty on Vercel so the frontend uses same-origin /api. Setting it to another host can cause custom-domain or CORS issues.
Build-time DB migration is enabled by default on Vercel production builds through scripts/vercel/build.cjs. Local builds do not run DB migrations unless asked explicitly.
offline_codex_bootstrap: Read docs/PROJECT_CONTEXT_EXPORT.md first for the full system map.
Treat README.md as the operator deployment runbook.
Start backend orientation from lib/api/router.js, then lib/api-handler.js, then lib/store.js.
Start frontend orientation from pages/[[...slug]].jsx, then frontend/src/App.js, then DashboardLayout, Settings, AdminSettings, PDFManagement, and SecureViewer.
Assume the live app is deployed as a single Vercel project using same-origin /api.
Do not assume the legacy backend/ folder is the active runtime unless the code explicitly references it.
Regenerate the context export before handoff if you change routes, env vars, schema, auth, billing, security, storage, translations, or team-workspace behavior.
operational_notes: Copy .env.example values into Vercel or local env files. Do not store real secrets in the context export.
Run npm install before local development.
Run npm run db:migrate when schema/index changes land.
Use npm run db:seed only when you intentionally want seed data.
Use npm run build before pushing structural changes.
Use npm run context:export after major changes to routes, env vars, schema, admin controls, storage, billing, or security flows.
Commit docs/PROJECT_CONTEXT_EXPORT.md and docs/PROJECT_CONTEXT_EXPORT.json with the related code changes so another Codex instance gets an accurate snapshot.

project_summary.deployment_model: Single Vercel project using Next.js pages plus a client-side React SPA mounted at the catch-all route.
legacy_areas: frontend/ (source assets and legacy CRA-era structure reused by the Next app)
backend/ (historical Python-era area, not the primary runtime)
project_summary.name: disposable-pdf-project
